sutta: the buddha said imagine monks a boil which has been gathering for many years it might have 9 gaping wounds 9 natural openings hence whatever might ooze out foulness would certainly ooze out stench would certainly ooze out loadsumners would certainly ooze out whatever might be discharged founders would certainly be discharged extension would certainly be discharged loadsumners would certainly be discharged a boil that is the name for the body monks made up of made up as it is of the 4 great elements procreated by mother and father built up of gruel and sour milk impermanent subject to irrational abrasion dissolution and disintegration with 9 gaping wounds 9 natural openings and from it whatever ooze out loudness certainly oozes out stench certainly oozes out loads certainly oozes out whatever be discharged foulness is certainly discharged stench is certainly discharged loadsomeness is certainly discharged therefore monks be you disgusted with this body as the end of the suta
commentary: you see here that the buddha's teaching is to be disgusted with the human body that the body is a load something with 9 openings or which discharges and dirty things all things but the worldly way nice to see the body as something attractive there's something exciting and here the buddha is certainly trying to tell us the opposite so that we can be free of suffering the nexus is
